markdown:
#  Break Down an Overall Project Budget in Portfolio Financials 
#####  Legacy
This information is intended for accounts with the [Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/) and [Capital Planning](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-capital-planning) products in Procore. Please reach out to your Procore point of contact for more information.
##  Background 
The Budget tab within the Cost Tracker allows you to build project budgets with variances and anticipated costs. There are two different budgeting methods that you can use in Portfolio Financials: a _broken down budget_ , which will allow you to assign budgets to individual cost items through the Cost Tracker, and an _overall (lump sum) budget_ , where you have one amount for the entire project. If you start with overall budget, you can choose to break it down later. However, if you start with a broken down budget, you will not be able to go back to an overall budget.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions** :
    * 'Full Access' to the project or higher.
  * **Additional Information** :
    * After you publish a broken down budget, you cannot go back to the overall project budgeting method. All future budgets for the project must be entered at the cost item or schedule of value level.


##  Prerequisites 
  * You must add cost items to the Cost Tracker before being able to break down your budget. See [Add a Cost Item to the Cost Tracker](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/tutorials/add-a-cost-item-to-the-cost-tracker-in-portfolio-financials).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the **Cost Tracker** section of the **Project Page**.
  2. Click the **vertical ellipsis** ![Icon Ellipsis Vertical](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b6d0fa56ca6a66941a4032499f56aec8/2bb6315cb1db00c907bda1e2ae7a081c/icon-vertical-ellipsis.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon on the Budget column and select **Break Down Budget**.  
  
![Break Down Budget](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2fa85e4e932ffe9237f67b3470a185c7/45fb65138cc9c5d31f293852addff5b5/break-down-budget.png?fm=webp)  

  3. On the 'Convert to Broken Down Budget' window, click **Continue**.
  4. Enter a **Name** and a **Description** (optional) for the broken down budget.
  5. Click **Continue**.
  6. On the Budget tab, you can now add a budget per cost item from the pre-existing budget in terms of gross dollar amount, cost per square foot, or variance (calculated as **_current draft budget_** minus (-) **_published budget_**).  
_Note_ : If you enter a value into one column, the other columns will calculate automatically. For example, if you enter a value into the cost per square foot column, the budget and variance columns will populate.
  7. After you have entered all values and are ready to publish the broken down budget, click **Publish**.


## See Also
  * [Add a Budget to a Project in Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/tutorials/add-a-budget-to-a-project-in-portfolio-financials)
  * [Portfolio Financials - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/permissions)
  * [Add a Revision to a Budget in Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/tutorials/add-a-revision-to-a-budget-in-portfolio-financials)
title: Break Down an Overall Project Budget in Portfolio Financials
description: How to break down an overall project budget that has been published for a project in Portfolio Financials.
